task_completion.description: Locate the Patient ID in the DICOM metadata.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent opens a DICOM metadata viewer, patient information panel in InVesalius, or uses a terminal tool to view the DICOM tags, revealing the Patient ID '4055947867'.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Bone' preset for the 3D surface model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The thresholding preset dropdown in the InVesalius interface shows 'Bone' selected.

task_completion.description: Create the 3D surface model of the skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a skull is rendered and visible in the 3D viewer pane of InVesalius.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to export the 3D surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog is open, initiated from InVesalius, for saving the 3D surface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Documents directory in the save dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the current directory set to '/home/ga/Documents' or 'Documents'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct filename with the Patient ID.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename field in the save dialog contains exactly '4055947867_Skull.stl'.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to create and export the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the InVesalius interface being actively used to generate the surface and export it.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to create a fake STL file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'touch' or 'echo' to create the STL file directly.

integrity.description: The export dialog belongs to InVesalius.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog is spawned from the InVesalius application, not a standalone text editor or file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct Patient ID from the DICOM metadata is '4055947867'. The agent must save the file exactly as '4055947867_Skull.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory. The file must be a genuine STL export from InVesalius.
